title: Hydroponic Screening Protocol for Salinity Tolerance in Galapagos Tomato Seedlings
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"k59n","text":"This protocol describes a method to evaluate salinity tolerance in Solanum cheesmaniae (Galapagos tomato) using a supported hydroponic system. It enables screening of a large number of genotypes for variation in sodium (Na⁺) exclusion capacity and growth responses under salt stress. The method includes standardized procedures for seed germination, hydroponic cultivation, salt treatment, and phenotypic evaluation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":67,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"3jjlf","text":"Important note: Avoid using green or yellow labels, pots, markers, or backgrounds to prevent interference with green pixel-based image analysis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Select 8 genotypes; sow 40 seeds per accession to allow for 6 replicates × 3 treatments × 2 seeds per pot, plus 2 extra pots per condition.
Sterilize trays, covers, pots, and plastic beads in bleach solution; rinse thoroughly.
Cut cheesecloth squares to line the bottom of each pot to retain beads.
Fill each pot with plastic beads and position in trays.
Add tap water to trays until the water reaches just below the bead surface.
Using a sterile 1 mL pipette tip, cut agar cylinders from MS plates and embed 3 per pot into the beads.
Surface sterilize seeds (20 min in 2.7% NaOCl), rinse well, and place one seed per agar plug.
Label trays and seal with tape. Place on nursery shelves and monitor daily.
Transfer trays to well-lit conditions once germination begins. Record germination dates.
Once cotyledons are fully expanded, transfer seedlings to the hydroponic system to avoid stress.
Mark desired water level in each hydroponic tank (e.g., 22 cm for ~100 L).
Determine number of EconoTray units required (each holds 118 pots).
Fill tanks with pre-filtered tap water and assess quality using a multi-ion electrode.
Add nutrients and salt solutions per treatment group. See Table 1 for CaCl₂ volumes to maintain Ca²⁺ activity at 0.4. Table 1. CaCl₂ Supplementation for Target NaCl Concentrations %s %s
Program pumps to cycle every 15 minutes (15 min on/off).
Monitor pH (~5.8), nutrient levels, and salt concentration weekly; refresh solution every 1–2 weeks.
Track emergence of the 4th leaf for initiating salt treatments.
Once the 4th leaf emerges, transfer seedlings from control tanks to salt treatment tanks.
Gradually increase salinity: 100 mM NaCl for 12 hours → 200 mM NaCl full treatment.
Power on camera, Photosimile lights, and EOS Utility software.
Remove pot label, place plant on Photosimile mark, and straighten it in the live view.
Capture image and replace label.
Prepare pre-labeled envelopes and tubes.
Record total number of true leaves (exclude cotyledons).
Remove plant by inverting pot and collecting beads. Avoid damaging roots.
Wash roots twice in 10 mM MgSO₄; pat dry with towel.
Measure root length; weigh fresh root biomass.
Cut root and store in envelope/tube.
Measure shoot length and fresh shoot weight.
Cut and scan the 4th leaf using WinFOLIA; save images with proper ID.
Separate leaf blades from petiole. Weigh blades; store tissues separately.
Place all samples in a 60°C drying oven for 48 hours.
Record dry weights of all tissue components.
creator: Yveline Pailles
